title: AbbVie Submits Rinvoq Applications for Vitiligo
text: AbbVie submitted applications to the FDA and EMA for a new Rinvoq indication in adult and adolescent patients with non-segmental vitiligo. The submissions were supported by Phase 3 Viti-Up study data in which upadacitinib met co-primary endpoints for total body and facial re-pigmentation at week 48. If approved, Rinvoq could become the first systemic medication for vitiligo, a chronic autoimmune disease with limited systemic treatment options. This news is important for AbbVie's investment story because Rinvoq is one of the company's key growth products as Humira declines after biosimilar competition. A successful label expansion would broaden Rinvoq's addressable market and support AbbVie's strategy of growing newer immunology medicines across multiple immune-mediated diseases.
